task_completion.description: Update the traffic light configuration to use actuated control.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the file /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio/pasubio_tls.add.xml where the <tlLogic> tags have been updated to include type="actuated".

task_completion.description: Add or update minDur and maxDur attributes for each phase based on the duration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for modifications to the <phase> tags in pasubio_tls.add.xml. Each phase should now have minDur (50% of duration, minimum 5) and maxDur (150% of duration) attributes. Note that some pre-existing minDur/maxDur values must be overwritten by the agent.

task_completion.description: Run the SUMO simulation using the provided configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the execution of the SUMO simulation (e.g., running `sumo -c run.sumocfg` or `sumo -c /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio/run.sumocfg`) and successfully generating the tripinfos.xml file.

task_completion.description: Generate the summary report file with the correct formatting keys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the creation or display of /home/ga/SUMO_Output/bus_travel_times.txt containing exactly the keys: 'bus_trips_completed:', 'avg_duration_s:', and 'avg_waitingTime_s:'.

task_completion.description: Calculate and report the correct statistics for the bus trips.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the values in bus_travel_times.txt. Because of a flaw in the task instructions (IDs do not contain 'bus', but vType does), accept 'bus_trips_completed: 0' (if the agent strictly filtered by ID) OR 'bus_trips_completed: 112' (if the agent smartly filtered by vType). The averages should correspond to the chosen filtering method (0/NaN if 0 trips).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not modify any phase states in the XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the pasubio_tls.add.xml file modifications to ensure the 'state="..."' strings inside the <phase> tags remain unchanged from their original values.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent parsed the XML data programmatically rather than hallucinating the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a Python script, shell command (like grep/awk), or similar programmatic method used to read tripinfos.xml and calculate the averages, rather than the agent just echoing hardcoded numbers into the text file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually ran the SUMO simulation to generate the tripinfos.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine SUMO terminal output logs (e.g., 'Step #...', 'Simulation ended at time...') rather than the agent manually creating a fake tripinfos.xml file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The working directory is /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio and output is /home/ga/SUMO_Output. The file pasubio_tls.add.xml already contains some predefined minDur and maxDur attributes; the agent MUST overwrite these to strictly follow the 50%/150% rule. CRITICAL SCORING NOTE: The task instructs the agent to filter trips where the vehicle ID contains 'bus'. However, in the provided tripinfos.xml, NO vehicle IDs contain 'bus' (they are formatted like '11_0'). The string 'bus' is actually in the 'vType' attribute (which yields 112 trips). If the agent strictly follows the prompt's literal instruction, it will output 0 completed trips. If it adapts and uses vType, it will output 112. Both approaches MUST be considered correct for the report content.
